identity.name: Resource
identity.definition: A Resource is a piece of instructional content — such as a digital book, article, video, interactive lesson, or practice set — available within Renaissance products that is aligned to specific skills/standards and used by students to learn, practice, and build mastery.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How a Resource appears in browsing grids and recommendation feeds

attributes.name: Title
attributes.description: Primary identifier. Appears on every card, search result, and assignment reference.

attributes.name: Resource Type
attributes.description: Book, Article, Video, Lesson, or Practice Set. Determines the icon and interaction model.

attributes.name: Subject
attributes.description: Subject area — ELA, Math, Science, Social Studies, SEL. Used for filtering and skill alignment.

attributes.name: Reading/Difficulty Level
attributes.description: Lexile, grade equivalent, or difficulty rating. Used for matching resources to student ability.

attributes.name: Product
attributes.description: Which Renaissance product hosts this resource — myON, Freckle, Nearpod, or Lalilo.

attributes.name: Title
attributes.description: Primary identifier. Appears on every card, search result, and assignment reference.

attributes.name: Resource Type
attributes.description: Book, Article, Video, Lesson, or Practice Set.

attributes.name: Subject
attributes.description: Subject area — ELA, Math, Science, Social Studies, SEL.

attributes.name: Reading/Difficulty Level
attributes.description: Lexile, grade equivalent, or difficulty rating.

attributes.name: Skill Alignment
attributes.description: Which Skills and Standards this resource is aligned to.

attributes.name: Product
attributes.description: Which Renaissance product hosts this resource.

attributes.name: Format
attributes.description: Text, Interactive, Video, or Audio.

attributes.name: Duration/Length
attributes.description: Page count, estimated reading time, or video length.

attributes.name: Publisher/Author
attributes.description: Content creator or publishing entity.

attributes.name: Topic/Genre
attributes.description: Genre or topic category — Fiction, Nonfiction, Science, Biography, etc. Multi-select.

attributes.name: Language
attributes.description: Content language — English, Spanish, French, etc.

attributes.name: Availability
attributes.description: Free, Licensed, Premium. Controls student access based on school/district subscription.

attributes.name: Status
attributes.description: Active or Archived. Archived resources are hidden from browse/search but retained for historical data.

stories.title: Browse and Read a Resource
stories.benefit: I can find content matched to my reading level and interests to build comprehension and vocabulary.
stories.whenClause: I search or browse the library,
stories.thenClause: I see resources filtered by my reading level, subject, and interests, and I can open any available resource to read, watch, or complete.

stories.title: Preview and Assign a Resource
stories.benefit: I can evaluate whether the content is appropriate and aligned to my learning objectives before students see it.
stories.whenClause: I preview a resource and choose to assign it,
stories.thenClause: I can select target classes or individual students, set a due date, and the assignment appears in each student's task list.

stories.title: Review Resource Usage
stories.benefit: I can understand which resources are engaging students and driving learning outcomes.
stories.whenClause: I view a resource's usage report,
stories.thenClause: I see which students have accessed it, completion rates, time spent, and any associated quiz or practice scores.

businessRules.title: Uniqueness
businessRules.description: A Resource is uniquely identified by its internal content ID within a product. The same content may exist as separate Resource instances across products (e.g., a book in myON and a lesson in Nearpod).

businessRules.title: Creation
businessRules.description: Resources are ingested via publisher feeds, content import pipelines, or internal authoring tools. Manual creation is limited to admin and content team roles.

businessRules.title: Skill Alignment
businessRules.description: Every Resource should be aligned to at least one Skill or Standard. Alignment is set during content import and can be refined by curriculum specialists.

businessRules.title: Level Matching
businessRules.description: When a Student browses resources, results are filtered or ranked by the student's current reading/difficulty level. Teachers can override level restrictions for specific assignments.

businessRules.title: Availability
businessRules.description: Resource availability is governed by the school or district license. Free resources are universally accessible; Licensed and Premium resources require an active subscription.

businessRules.title: Archival
businessRules.description: When a Resource is archived, it is hidden from browse and search results but remains accessible in historical assignment and score records. Archived resources cannot be newly assigned.

businessRules.title: Usage Tracking
businessRules.description: Every student interaction with a Resource (open, read/play, complete, bookmark) generates a usage event. These events feed into usage reports and inform adaptive recommendations.

states.name: Active
states.description: Available for browsing, searching, and assignment

states.name: Archived
states.description: Hidden from discovery but retained for historical data
states.triggers: Admin action, license expiration, publisher withdrawal

relationships.mechanics: Students access Resources through browsing, search, or teacher-assigned work. Usage events (open, read, complete) are tracked per student per resource.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many. A Resource can be accessed by many Students; a Student interacts with many Resources. Typical: hundreds of students per popular resource.
relationships.sorts: Resources for a Student: by reading level match (default), by recently accessed, by subject, alphabetically by title.
relationships.filters: By subject, by resource type, by reading level range, by product, by completion status (not started, in progress, completed).
relationships.dependencies: If a Student is deactivated, their usage history for the Resource persists for reporting but the student no longer appears in active usage lists.

relationships.mechanics: Resources are aligned to Skills during content import. A resource may target multiple skills; completion contributes to skill proficiency tracking.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many. A Resource aligns to multiple Skills; a Skill is practiced through multiple Resources.
relationships.sorts: Skills for a Resource: by alignment strength (primary/secondary), alphabetically.
relationships.filters: By standard framework (Common Core, state standards), by subject domain, by grade band.
relationships.dependencies: If a Skill is deprecated or remapped, resource alignments are updated in bulk by curriculum team.

relationships.mechanics: Teachers assign Resources to students or classes, creating Assignment instances. A resource can be assigned multiple times to different groups.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. A Resource can be referenced by many Assignments; each Assignment references one Resource (or a set in a playlist).
relationships.sorts: Assignments for a Resource: by creation date (newest first), by due date, by class.
relationships.filters: By class, by assignment status (active, completed, overdue), by teacher.
relationships.dependencies: If a Resource is archived, existing assignments remain accessible for completion but no new assignments can reference the archived resource.

nestedObjects.name: Skill / Standard
nestedObjects.description: Skills and standards this resource is aligned to

nestedObjects.name: Score
nestedObjects.description: Quiz/practice scores generated from this resource

nestedObjects.name: Quiz
nestedObjects.description: Embedded quizzes or comprehension checks within the resource

allAttributes.name: Title
allAttributes.description: Primary identifier. Appears on every card, search result, and assignment reference.

allAttributes.name: Resource Type
allAttributes.description: Book, Article, Video, Lesson, or Practice Set. Determines the icon, interaction model, and default viewer.

allAttributes.name: Subject
allAttributes.description: Subject area — ELA, Math, Science, Social Studies, SEL. Used for filtering and skill alignment.

allAttributes.name: Reading/Difficulty Level
allAttributes.description: Lexile measure, grade equivalent, or difficulty rating. Used for matching resources to student ability.

allAttributes.name: Skill Alignment
allAttributes.description: Which Skills and Standards this resource is aligned to. Drives adaptive recommendations.

allAttributes.name: Product
allAttributes.description: Which Renaissance product hosts this resource — myON, AR, Freckle, Nearpod, or Lalilo.

allAttributes.name: Format
allAttributes.description: Text, Interactive, Video, or Audio. Determines the rendering/playback approach.

allAttributes.name: Duration/Length
allAttributes.description: Page count for books, estimated time for articles/videos, question count for practice sets.

allAttributes.name: Publisher/Author
allAttributes.description: Content creator or publishing entity. Shown on detail views and in catalog metadata.

allAttributes.name: Topic/Genre
allAttributes.description: Genre or topic category — Fiction, Nonfiction, Science, Biography, Poetry, etc. Multi-select for cross-genre content.

allAttributes.name: Language
allAttributes.description: Content language — English, Spanish, French, etc. Used for language-specific browsing and Lalilo content.

allAttributes.name: Availability
allAttributes.description: Free, Licensed, or Premium. Controls student access based on school/district subscription tier.

allAttributes.name: Status
allAttributes.description: Active or Archived. Archived resources are hidden from browse/search but retained for historical data.

structure.evidence: A Resource has rich attributes: title, type, subject, reading level, skill alignment, product, format, duration, publisher, topic/genre, language, availability, and status. You can design a full detail page.

instances.evidence: Charlotte's Web (680L Book on myON), Fraction Foundations (Grade 4 Practice Set on Freckle), Volcanoes: Earth's Fiery Power (820L Article on myON). Libraries contain thousands of Resource instances.

purpose.evidence: Resources are the core content students consume. Students browse and read them, teachers preview and assign them, and the system tracks usage to inform adaptive learning paths. Without Resources, there is nothing for students to learn from.

sipValidation.verdict: Core knowledge object. The foundational content unit across all learning products.

synonyms.notes: Specifically refers to digital or physical book resources
Short-form text content — news articles, informational passages
Structured instructional sequence with multiple activities
Generic term used in platform architecture and APIs
Used in professional development and curriculum planning contexts
Interactive practice exercises and skill-building tasks

objectViews.description: A browsable grid of resources filtered by subject and level. Teachers scan covers and titles to find content for upcoming lessons, with quick access to preview or favorite.
Resources matching a search query with type, subject, level, and format. Teachers narrow down options and preview content before selecting.
Resources listed for selection when building an assignment. Teachers preview content and assign it to students directly from here.
The complete resource record — metadata, skill alignment, publisher, availability, and all actions. Teachers review a resource fully before assigning, favoriting, or checking usage.
Resources a student has read or interacted with, listed with difficulty level and duration. Teachers track reading volume and level progression over time.
Recommended resources shown as cover cards based on student level and interests. Teachers browse personalized suggestions to find the right content for their students.
